IBM.rationale: IBM's valuation is stretched (P/E 72.8x, P/B 5.4x) on weak fundamentals (net margin 2.7%, revenue declining, EPS volatile), but the recent rally off the March lows and positive news flow on quantum/AI provide a mild tactical tailwind that limits downside risk over a year.
key_factors: P/E diluted 72.8x vs 1y range 19.2-107.9x — elevated but not at peak
Revenue trajectory: FY2022 $60.53B with quarterly EPS volatile ($2.99, -$3.54, $1.53) — fundamental quality is poor
Net margin 2.7%, operating margin 1.9%, ROE 7.4% — thin profitability
Price recovered from $123.28 (3/15) to $131.09 (3/31), +6.3% in two weeks on positive news (quantum center, rally)
1y return only +0.8% despite rally — suggests limited forward momentum

sources_cited.excerpt: IBM Unveils Quantum Computing Research Center in Spain

HD.rationale: Reasonable valuation and high-quality franchise at a multi-year low offer a mild long lean, but sequential quarterly revenue/earnings deceleration from the housing slowdown caps upside conviction.
key_factors: P/E 17.7x sits in middle of 1y range (16.2-20.5x), not stretched but not deeply discounted
Quarterly revenue decelerating: $43.79B (Q2) → $38.87B (Q3) → $35.83B (Q4 FY22), reflecting housing slowdown
Stock down 6.6% over 3 months to $295.12, near multi-year lows per news framing
Quality franchise with 15.3% operating margin and 33.5% gross margin, FY22 EPS $16.69 on $157.4B revenue
Mixed news sentiment: bullish 'buy before bull market' pieces offset by downgrades and housing headwinds
